mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in > 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during tightening cycles or returns are negative in > 55% of positive growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5 percent annually forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation months or policy response is delayed beyond 3 months without equity drawdown.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative returns in > 50% of observations or interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in multivariate tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased construction activity and consumer confidence in long-term economic stability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or housing starts growth fails to predict return direction with > 55% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates severe supply-side contraction and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative threshold events produce positive returns in > 55% of cases or predictive accuracy drops below chance levels in walk-forward testing.
